title: GBP Post Removed by Google Policy Violation
description:
Seeking help w/ the client request below. I will be adding the ticket verbatim to be sure:


Summary: Google removed a GBP post from the Grover Law Firm profile on May 25, 2026 citing a violation of their content policy for "abusive or low quality content." The removed post appears to be related to passenger car accident content. Steve Grover (Principal) forwarded the Google notification to David Bloomfield (Marketing Manager) who flagged it to Clixsy. The team needs to identify which post was removed, understand why it was flagged, and determine next steps.


Client Goal: Identify the removed GBP post, understand why it was flagged by Google, and determine whether it can be revised and reposted in compliance with Google's content policy.


Requirements for Completion:

  • Removed GBP post is identified (partial content from Google's notification: "Being injured as a passenger in a car accident can leave you overwhelmed with medical concerns...")
  • Root cause of the removal is assessed against Google's posts content policy
  • A revised version of the post is created that complies with Google's content policy if reposting is appropriate
  • Revised post is submitted to the GBP listing
  • SEO to provide David can be updated

Deadline: No hard deadline stated, but Steve Grover (Principal) is aware and forwarded this directly — treat as a priority.


Priority: MEDIUM — The GBP is still functioning and the firm is not losing leads directly from this removal. However, a principal-level contact is watching this and it should be resolved by end of next business day.


Thanks much!

Ysanna Ironstand:
As a Client Intake Coordinator, Ysanna provides a professional and compassionate first point of contact for clients seeking legal assistance. She strive’s to understand each client’s situation, gathering key details, and guiding them through the intake process with care and efficiency.
Ysanna was raised in Saskatchewan and moved to Calgary five years ago. Outside of her professional life, she enjoys bonding with friends and family, camping, and hiking in the mountains. She indulges by relaxing with a puzzle and a good TV show or a movie. 


Ana Solorzano:
Ana is a Client Intake Coordinator with a background in paralegal studies and knowledge of personal injury law. She is organized, detail-oriented, and focused on providing a positive experience for clients as their first point of contact.

She is passionate about helping people feel supported during difficult situations and committed to delivering efficient and professional service. Outside of work, Ana enjoys spending time with her family and caring for her puppy.

David Bloomfield:
Known for his grit and cross-functional problem-solving mindset, David loves the challenge in complex environments-aligning marketing, operations, and client experience to deliver measurable results. David brings a gritty, no-nonsense approach to growth. He is known for pushing past conventional marketing playbooks, testing harder, and finding opportunities. Whether it's launching new markets, scaling growth, or tightening conversion pipelines. He operates with urgency, accountability, and an edge that delivers. At his core, David is a builder of systems, partnerships, and momentum.

Outside the office, David is a proud father of two boys and devoted husband to his wife Kim, who he's loved since grade six. Family is at the core of everything he does, grounding his work ethic, shaping his values, and driving the same loyalty and commitment he brings to his team and clients every day.

title: Attorney Bio Updates
description:
Grover wants to update the latest attorney profiles and profile picture for the follow attorneys:

SEAN MIHALCHEON
JEANELLE CROSSER
NICOLAS CONSTANTINI
MARIA DOUCETTE - LEGAL ASSISTANT ( NEW STAFF)

https://www.groverlawfirm.com/about-our-firm/


SEAN MIHALCHEON:
Sean is an experienced litigator with a background in Civil Litigation and Criminal Defence. Prior to joining the Grover Law team, Sean operated his own full service general law practice which exposed him to various areas of law, including Wills and Estates, Civil and Criminal Litigation, Employment Law, Human Rights Law, and Constitutional Law, among others. Sean also became one of a handful of lawyers in Alberta specialized in Covid-19 litigation during the height of the pandemic.  
 
Prior to undertaking law school, Sean graduated from Mount Royal University with a History degree in 2014. He then attended the University of Saskatchewan where he graduated with a Juris Doctor law degree in 2019, before being called to the bar in Alberta in 2020. With over 20 years of experience in the Canadian Army and a combat tour to Afghanistan in 2008, Sean brings a pragmatic, yet strategic approach to resolving client matters. Likewise, Sean is always prepared to appear in Court to advocate on behalf of his clients to ensure their rights and interests are being protected.
 
Sean is a member of the Calgary Bar Association, the Canadian Bar Association (CBA), the Alberta Civil Trial Lawyers Association (ACTLA), the Academy of Truck Accident Attorneys (ATAA), and the American Association for Justice (AAJ). Outside of work, Sean enjoys spending quality time with friends and family, travelling, and being outdoors. He also has a keen interest in classic Simpsons trivia, Scotch, and Lego.

JEANELLE CROSSER:
Jeanelle comes to Grove Law with over 15 years of office management experience in the
legal industry. She has worked with several mid-sized law firms, and her extensive
knowledge and hands-on experience have been a valuable asset to the firm.
In her previous roles, Jeanelle successfully led two major office relocation projects. Along
with her strong organizational abilities, she is known for her excellent interpersonal skills
and her ability to remain calm and solution focused when handling challenging situations.
Her loyalty and dedication to the firm and its team never go unnoticed.
Outside of work, Jeanelle enjoys exploring culinary arts, spending time with her family, and
entertaining friends.

NICOLAS CONSTANTINI:
Nicolas Constantini is a dedicated Legal Assistant who earned his Legal Assistant Diploma in 2022, bringing a strong academic foundation to his work in the legal field.
Prior to joining the firm, Nicolas accumulated four years of experience in the real estate industry, where he developed sharp attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and the ability to navigate complex documentation — competencies that translate seamlessly into his role supporting legal professionals.
Outside of his professional life, Nicolas has a deep appreciation for the arts and creative pursuits, which foster his natural ability to think critically, solve complex problems, and approach challenges with an inventive and resourceful mindset.

MARIA DOUCETTE - LEGAL ASSISTANT ( NEW STAFF):
Maria is a detailed-orientated Legal Assistant with over 8 years of experience at a law firms, focusing on personal injury cases. Maria supports clients through litigation and focusing on making the legal process less intimidating. She is deeply committed to accuracy and client's success. Maria is fluent in  English, Russian and Belarussian, allowing her  to serve a diverse clientele with a valuable global perspective. When not at work, Maria spends time doing activities with her family, she loves to travel, hit tennis court and explore hiking trails.

Thank you!

Please update the Grover Law Firm "Our Firm" page (https://www.groverlawfirm.com/about-our-firm/).

Add new members:
- They do not have photos yet. Please use the placeholder images.


1. Mary Tagulao (Legal Assistant)
Bio:  
Mary Tagulao is a dedicated and versatile professional who earned her Paralegal Diploma from Reeves College in 2024. She also studied Web-End Development at the University of Calgary, adding a valuable technical edge to her diverse skill set.

With several years of experience as a Casino Surveillance Officer, Mary has honed her strong observational and analytical skills—qualities that enhance her effectiveness in both legal and technical environments.

Outside of her career, Mary finds joy and tranquility in the company of her cherished dogs and birds. Her deep love for animals brings balance, inspiration, and happiness to her everyday life.
-----

2.  Kirnpreet Singh (Articling Student)
Bio:
Kirnpreet is a student-at-law at Grover Law and completed her Juris Doctor degree at Thompson Rivers University (TRU). Prior to law school, she graduated from the University of Calgary with a Bachelor of Arts in Law and Society.
During law school, she served as a student clinician with the TRU Community Legal Clinic and volunteered with Pro Bono Students Canada, where is provided accessible legal support to underrepresented groups. In this role, she enjoyed fostering relationships and giving back to her community.

Outside of the office, Kirnpreet enjoys spending quality time with friends and family, staying active, and trying new brunch places.
-----

Add attorney bios:
  
1. Nikita Sharma
Nikita Sharma serves as a Legal Assistant at Grover Law Firm, where she provides dedicated support to thePersonal Injury matters. She assists with research, file management, document preparation and client communication, helping ensure that each case progresses smoothly and that clients feel supported throughout the legal process.

A qualified advocate in India, Nikita holds an LL.M. in Criminal Justice from Queen Mary University of London, United Kingdom. With over five years of experience in litigation, policy research, and advocacy, she brings a strong legal foundation and a compassionate, detail-oriented approach to her role. She is additionally pursuing NCA accreditation to become a Solicitor & Barrister in Canada.

Languages Spoken: English, Hindi, and Punjabi 
-----

2.  Robin Shea
Originally from Ontario, Robin moved abroad as a child and feels that it granted her a special appreciation for diversity and different cultures. With a background in customer service and sales, Robin achieved academic excellence in her completion of a Paralegal Diploma in 2025 and has a love for all things related to our legal system. Robin has a passion for music and specifically, singing, playing guitar, songwriting and the recording process. Robin also enjoys spending time with loved ones, playing racquet sports and watching sci-fi shows and comedies. 
-----

3.  Peter Robertson
Peter Robertson was born in Digby, Nova Scotia, where he began his career working alongside his father at Canadian Tire. Through this early experience, he developed a passion for the automotive industry. While learning the ropes in that field, he also trained and worked as a paramedic.

Peter's entrepreneurial spirit led him to launch several businesses, including a Sears outlet, 3TP Promotions, and a clothing company. He was also a co-founder of the Wharf Rat Rally, which became Canada’s largest multi-day motorcycle rally. After noticing a post-COVID decline in the event's momentum, Peter, along with fellow bikers, initiated a new event called Sou’West Iron and Ink to help revitalize and enhance the rally experience

In 2010, Peter moved to Alberta to further his career in the automotive sector, eventually managing a large dealership. During his time in Fairview, Alberta, he served a term on the town council and remained actively involved in community life, supporting local youth through Little League, minor hockey, and various festivals and events.

In 2019, Peter returned to Nova Scotia, continuing to support community events and biker culture through his involvement in the Wharf Rat Rally and the new Sou'West Iron and Ink initiative.Peter is the proud father of two children, Tarah and Tyler. He enjoys motorcycling, curling, fishing, kayaking, and hockey. In November 2024, he moved back to Alberta with his partner Stacy to accept a position as Marketing Manager at Grover Law Firm. 
-----

4.  Jack Matheson
Jack Matheson is a marketing professional with over six years of experience, specializing in digital strategy, content creation, and brand development. He completed his education at SAIT, where he built a strong foundation in marketing and business. Outside of work, he enjoys staying active through physical activities like hiking and sports, and he is passionate about continuous learning, always seeking new ways to expand his skills and knowledge. 
----

Thanks much!

In this episode, we sit down with Tim Sheriff, a seasoned trucking lawyer based in North Carolina, whose journey from police officer to legal advocate is nothing short of inspiring. Tim shares his unique insights into the trucking industry, the challenges drivers face on the road, and the importance of legal representation in ensuring safety and compliance.

title: Prepare the Nova Scotia silo strategy
description:
The Grover team would like us to prepare a silo strategy for the province of Nova Scotia and materials so they can see great traction by June. (They have an event in June)

Here is some context from the last Grover Call:
Corey explained the strategic approach using Farah and Farah (a Florida law firm) as an example. He described how they structure their website with content silos by state/province rather than trying to expand the motorcycle website into new territories. The key insight was that the Grover Law Firm domain should be the central hub for expansion, not the motorcycle website, because the law firm can build topical authority across multiple practice areas, while the motorcycle site is limited to motorcycle-specific 

They would like to monitor the silos for this month.

Requirements Checklist
  • Start building these silos right away
  • Target keywords like "Yukon motorcycle lawyer" to rank #1
  • High-value cases, despite a smaller population (uncharted territory with high accident values)

title: Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
description:
Hi, this is inline w/ a group of tasks we have from a sheet. Here is the link for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.


you can use this as robots.txt:

# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---

Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google 
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.goarco.com/sitemap_index.xml

description:
Alberta introduced new rules for obtaining a Class 6 motorcycle license aimed at improving road safety and streamlining the licensing process. The most notable change is the removal of the second advanced road test for full licensure, replacing it with a 2-year probationary period for new riders. During this period, it's crucial to maintain a clean record with no suspensions or tickets in the final 12 months. Additionally, the basic road test has been extended by 15 minutes to thoroughly assess key riding skills such as defensive driving, traffic navigation, and emergency handling.

At Grover Law Firm, we’re dedicated to ensuring that motorcyclists in Alberta stay informed and prepared for these changes. Whether you’re a new rider or upgrading to a full Class 6 license, it's important to stay updated on these rules. title: Send Welcome Email
list_title: ONBOARDING › Pre-Discovery ADMIN / ACCOUNT MANAGER / PM
description:
Dear XXXXX (Client Contact),

On behalf of the entire Clixsy staff, I’d like to take this opportunity to welcome you as a new client. We are thrilled to have you with us.

I am also happy to say that I will be your primary point of contact, and please get in touch at any time with your questions, comments and feedback.

NEW CLIENT INTAKE FORM
 
I have linked you to our New Client Intake Form, below. Can you, or a designated individual, please fill it in and submit at your earliest convenience?
 
This New Client Intake Form forms the basis for our Discovery and initial kick-off meeting in which we’ll get together to begin working together and establishing roles, responsibilities and expectations. 
 
Please ensure you thoroughly fill in what you can, and if you are not able to fill something in, just let us know and we’ll work with you to either obtain the information or determine whether it’s a priority at this time. 
 
There are sections with requests for technical information like usernames and passwords for your online assets. Please try to be as accurate as possible with these so we can get in and audit your website/social/advertising accounts without having to return to you with further requests. This may slow the process up and cost us valuable time.
 
 Don’t worry if you can’t, or are unwilling to disclose all the information we have requested as the main goal here is to have at hand any resources we’ll need to work efficiently and to ensure that we organize and maintain current/accurate information for you so that you’ll never have to say “I don’t know” when a vendor or internal partner/employee asks “who has the login to XYZ”. Not to mention any information you can supply will help get the best returns we can from your investment with us. Just try and be as accurate and as thorough as you can. We will cover everything in more depth at your Discovery Meeting. 
 
http://www.clixsy.com/intake/

DISCOVERY MEETING
 
As soon as we have received and processed your New Client Intake Form, I will reach out to find a time to hold your Discovery Meeting. This will be held online via a Zoom call. 

 In the Discovery Meeting we will answer any questions you may have and talk about who on our team addresses the various roles and responsibilities, what next steps will be and establish our “battle rhythm” as our teams determine how best to work in unison. We will use the New Client Intake Form to discuss things like, your business and campaign goals, KPIs, customer journeys through your digital assets, your competitors etc. We will brainstorm the best methods to reach your goals and agree a ‘Definition of DONE’, for each stage of the process. 
 
We will also discuss any legal advertising constraints there may be in your industry and set boundaries based on what your company culture is comfortable with, and what is appropriate in the market. 
 
This should give us all the information we need to get started our work in increasing your market share and reaching your business goals. 
 
Once again, thank you for entrusting Clixsy with your business. Together we'll do our best to get you as big an ROI and slice of the market share as we can, as quickly as possible,
 
 
 Best regards,
 
 
 
 R. Corey Vandenberg
